questions.question: Which country is the largest seafood exporter globally?
questions.explanation: China leads the world in seafood exports with an estimated $24.8 billion USD in 2025. China has the largest aquaculture production worldwide, including major species like tilapia, carp, and shrimp. China also processes seafood for re-export in global markets. Norway, Vietnam, and Ecuador follow China in global seafood export rankings based on recent international trade data.

questions.question: Which category contributes most to global e-waste generation?
questions.explanation: In 2022, global e-waste generation reached 62 million tonnes according to the Global E-waste Monitor 2024. Computer and telecommunication equipment account for a major portion, including personal computers, laptops, mobile phones, and telecommunications infrastructure. These items have high turnover rates and are widely used worldwide. Large household appliances and other electrical equipment contribute significant amounts, but computing and telecom devices remain the largest single category.

questions.question: What is Odisha’s share in India’s total bauxite production?
questions.explanation: Odisha produces 76% of India’s bauxite. In 2020–21, Odisha produced 20,379 thousand tonnes of bauxite, accounting for 76.3% of the national output. Odisha has 59% of India’s total bauxite reserves. Bauxite is the main source of aluminium, and Odisha is the top producer of this ore in India.

questions.question: Which country has the largest number of internet users globally?
questions.explanation: In 2023, China had about 1.08 billion internet users. India had nearly 850 million users, the United States about 312 million, and Indonesia over 200 million. China’s internet population is the world’s largest due to its overall population of approximately 1.4 billion and national internet expansion since the 2000s. The China Internet Network Information Center provides official user statistics.

questions.question: Which field had the most research publications in India in 2020?
questions.explanation: In 2020, Computer & Information Science accounted for 18% of India’s total research publications. Engineering contributed 17%, Health Sciences 16%, Biological & Biomedical Sciences 12%, Physics 11%, and Chemistry 8%. Data is from NSF Science & Engineering Indicators 2022. Computer & Information Science represented the largest share among all fields in 2020 for India.

questions.question: What is capital Indexed Bonds?

options.label: B
options.text: The interest rate depends on Bank rate

options.label: C
options.text: The interest rate depends on WPI or CPI

options.label: D
options.text: The interest rate depends on Repo Rate

answer.label: C
answer.text: The interest rate depends on WPI or CPI

questions.explanation: The capital Indexed bonds are the bonds where the interest rates is a fixed percentage over the inflation rate. It is depending on either WPI or CPI depending on the terms.

questions.question: Which projects are primarily funded by REITs?
questions.explanation: REITs mainly invest in completed, income-generating real estate assets such as operational commercial properties. Income is generated from rent paid by tenants in these properties. Regulatory guidelines require REITs in India to invest at least 80% of their assets in completed and revenue-generating properties, as per SEBI norms notified in 2014. They do not generally fund under-construction or greenfield projects.

questions.question: Government will cover up to how much percentage of project costs through National Clean Energy Fund?
questions.explanation: The National Clean Energy Fund was started with the carbon tax collected through clean energy cess. The proceeds are used for funding clean energy projects. Government will provide financial support upto 40% of the total project cost

questions.question: Who is the first Asian woman to get a PhD in Economics from Harvard back in 1960?
questions.explanation: The first Asian woman to get a PhD in Economics from Harvard back in 1960 is Padma Desai. She is best known for a critique of India’s erstwhile planned economy. She co-authored a book in early 1970’s with husband and fellow economist Jagdish Bhagwati, on India’s industrial and trade policies.

questions.question: Which is the longest canal in India?
questions.explanation: The Indira Gandhi Canal has a total length of approximately 650 km. It starts at Harike Barrage in Punjab and runs through Rajasthan, primarily for irrigation in the Thar Desert. The main canal is about 445 km and the feeder canal is about 204 km. Construction began in 1952 and major phases completed by 2010.
